data.title: Solana Achieves Record Stablecoin Transaction Volume in February
data.description:
In February, Solana's blockchain recorded stablecoin transactions totaling $650 billion, marking a historic high and the highest level among all blockchains for the month. According to Odaily, this figure more than doubled the previous peak from October last year. A research report by Grayscale Investments, using data from Allium, attributes the surge to increased retail demand for on-chain payments, which has significantly boosted stablecoin activity. The report highlights Solana's shift from primarily meme coin transactions to SOL and stablecoin trading pairs, indicating a rise in payment use cases.

Standard Chartered previously noted that Solana's low transaction costs are aiding its expansion into micropayments and native internet financial applications. In terms of market share, Solana currently ranks as the fourth largest stablecoin supplier across all networks and is second only to Ethereum in USDC circulation. Analysts suggest that while Ethereum continues to dominate the stablecoin and real-world asset sectors, stablecoins may become a crucial pillar for the maturity of the Solana network.

data.title: Middle East Conflict May Impact Italy's Economic Growth, ING Analyst Warns
data.description: According to Jin10, Paolo Pizzoli from ING highlights in a report that the escalating Middle East conflict could negatively affect Italy's economic growth this year. In the fourth quarter of 2025, Italy's GDP grew by 0.3% quarter-on-quarter. Pizzoli states that the future growth prospects for Italy's economy will largely depend on the developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict and the broader Middle East situation, as well as their spillover effects on the energy market. He notes that Italy's reliance on energy imports is relatively high, and Italian companies are already paying higher energy prices compared to most European counterparts. The Iranian conflict and its ripple effects on the energy market could significantly slow economic growth in the first half of 2026, making the previous annual growth forecast of 0.8% appear overly optimistic.

data.title: Doppler and Hex Trust Collaborate to Expand Wrapped XRP Infrastructure
data.description: Doppler has partnered with Hex Trust to enhance the custody and yield infrastructure for Wrapped XRP. According to Foresight News, this collaboration aims to extend XRP liquidity beyond the XRP Ledger to multiple blockchain ecosystems. The initiative is expected to facilitate broader access and integration of XRP across various platforms.

data.title: Fabric Protocol (ROBO) Spot Listing Alters Alpha Trading Competition Schedule
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X that the Alpha trading competition will conclude earlier than initially planned due to the spot listing of Fabric Protocol (ROBO). The competition is now set to end on March 4 at 15:59 (UTC). This adjustment in schedule means that winners will be determined based on their trading volume at the time of the event's conclusion.

The rewards for the competition will be distributed on a pro-rata basis, reflecting the actual duration of the competition. Participants are encouraged to check the latest Trading Competition announcement for updated details regarding the event. This change underscores the dynamic nature of trading competitions and the need for participants to stay informed about any modifications that may arise due to new listings or other factors.

data.title: Gold and silver prices rose across the board, while cryptocurrency volatility indices fell, with both the BVIX and EVIX declining by more than 0.4% intraday.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $5,180.81 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.81%. Silver prices rose to $84.956 per ounce, a daily increase of 3.58%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 55.09, a daily decrease of 0.58%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 75.08, a daily increase of 0.47%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate fell 0.24% to 6.89829. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.23% to 157.316. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 rose 0.73% to 5,864.39 points; the UK FTSE 100 rose 0.48% to 10,566.65 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.51% to 24,141.63 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil fell 0.36% to $74.62 per barrel, and Brent crude oil fell 0.32% to $82.00 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Over $75 million in margin calls were triggered across the entire network in the past hour, primarily affecting short positions.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $75.86 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $73.7 million in short positions and $2.15 million in long positions. In addition, $58.4 million in BTC positions were liquidated and $8.91 million in ETH positions were liquidated.

data.title: Incident Reported 20 Nautical Miles West of Dubai
data.description: An incident has been reported 20 nautical miles west of Dubai, according to the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO). Details surrounding the event remain unclear, and further information is awaited to understand the nature and impact of the incident. UKMTO advises vessels in the vicinity to exercise caution and remain vigilant. The situation is being monitored closely, and updates will be provided as more information becomes available.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Predicts Extended Conflict with Iran
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has indicated that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran could extend for several weeks. According to Jin10, Hegseth stated that the duration of the conflict might range from four to eight weeks or even longer. He emphasized that the U.S. will control the pace and intensity of the operations.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Gains as Tech Stocks Rally
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with gains, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising by 0.37%, the S&P 500 increasing by 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbing by 0.45%. According to Jin10, prominent technology stocks saw widespread gains. Micron Technology surged nearly 4%, ASML Holding advanced by 2.6%, and both Tesla and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company rose by over 1%.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 72,000 USDT with a 7.10% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 04, 2026, 14:31 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 72,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 72,180.710938 USDT, with a narrowed 7.10% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Proposes Ending Seniority Protections in Federal Layoffs
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has proposed a significant change to the federal workforce policy by suggesting the removal of seniority protections during layoffs. According to Jin10, this move aims to streamline the process and potentially prioritize performance over tenure. The proposal has sparked discussions among policymakers and federal employees, as it could alter the dynamics of job security within government positions. Critics argue that removing seniority protections might lead to increased uncertainty for long-serving employees, while supporters believe it could enhance efficiency and meritocracy in the federal workforce. The debate continues as stakeholders assess the potential impacts of this policy shift.

data.title: Ford's February U.S. Auto Production and Electric Vehicle Sales Figures Released
data.description: Ford Motor Company reported its U.S. auto production for February, reaching a total of 188,197 vehicles. According to Jin10, the company also announced that its electric vehicle sales in the U.S. for the same month totaled 14,132 units. This data highlights Ford's ongoing efforts to expand its presence in the electric vehicle market while maintaining robust overall production numbers.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin shows signs of recovery after five consecutive days of decline; healthy expansion of derivatives points to a phase of recovery.
data.description: A Bitfinex report points out that Bitcoin has experienced five consecutive months of decline since October 2025, marking the first time since 2018 that it has formed a "five-month losing streak." February alone saw a 14.93% drop, with a cumulative maximum drawdown of approximately 52.34%. However, early signs of recovery emerged in March. Data shows that since March 1st, approximately $3.2 billion worth of BTC has been systematically bought at market prices across exchanges, successfully recovering the $65,000 mark. The Coinbase premium index ended 40 consecutive days of negative values ​​and turned positive, indicating a return of spot buying from the US. The derivatives structure also remains relatively healthy: open interest rose to $53.1 billion, a 15.4% increase from Sunday's close, but the perpetual funding rate was only about 9.5% APR, showing no signs of overheating. OI (Online Inflow) and spot trading expanded in tandem, reflecting that this round of gains was largely driven by spot market absorption. Regarding ETFs, the US spot Bitcoin ETF recorded a net inflow of approximately $1.1 billion last week, with over $450 million flowing in on Monday and Tuesday combined, indicating that institutional demand remains the core support. Analysts believe that if key support levels hold, Bitcoin may recover to the $80,000-$85,000 range within the next 1-3 months; in the short term, attention should be paid to the $72,000-$74,000 area of ​​concentrated short selling and the potential dynamic support level of $66,000. The overall assessment remains cautiously bullish.

data.title: An insider wallet, suspected of being linked to the US-Iran conflict, invested $3,000 to predict the Iranian regime would collapse before March 31.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, an insider wallet suspected of being linked to the US-Iran conflict, after three days of inactivity, invested $3,000 to predict that the Iranian regime would collapse before March 31. The wallet was created on February 28 and had only made two prediction trades before, both of which were successful, with a profit of $16,000.

data.title: The UK House of Lords questioned Coinbase executives about the risks of stablecoins.
data.description: The UK House of Lords questioned Tom Duff Gordon, Coinbase's Vice President of International Policy, on Wednesday, focusing on whether stablecoins could lead to a loss of bank deposits and pose new risks to the UK financial system. Gordon stated that fully reserve and regulated stablecoins are "safer than uninsured bank deposits" because they are backed 1:1 by cash and high-quality government securities and are redeemable at face value. He emphasized that stablecoins can significantly reduce payment costs and accelerate cross-border payments. Members of the House of Lords questioned the redemption risks of stablecoins during a crisis, their potential impact on banks, and their role in criminal activities. Gordon countered that concerns about bank disintermediation were "grossly exaggerated" and stressed that Coinbase strictly enforces KYC and anti-money laundering measures. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Study Highlights Impact of Aid Cuts on Child Marriage Progress
data.description: A recent study, supported by former Meta executive Sheryl Sandberg, reveals that reductions in humanitarian and development aid may hinder or reverse advancements in ending child marriages. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential consequences of these funding cuts on global efforts to combat child marriage. The study emphasizes the importance of sustained financial support to maintain progress in this area. Without adequate resources, initiatives aimed at preventing child marriages could face significant setbacks, affecting millions of young girls worldwide. The findings underscore the critical role of international aid in addressing this pressing issue.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Mary Daly on Inflation and Labor Market Outlook
data.description: Federal Reserve Board member Mary Daly has expressed that the market does not seem concerned about long-term inflation expectations. According to NS3.AI, Daly also noted that it is premature to confirm any weakening trend in the labor market. Her comments suggest ongoing vigilance but no immediate alarm regarding inflation or labor market conditions.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Advocates for Interest Rate Cut
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan has suggested that a one percent interest rate reduction this year would be suitable. According to NS3.AI, he argues that the current rates are approximately one percent above the neutral rate, indicating a possible shift towards a more accommodative monetary policy soon.

data.title: Federal Reserve Plans Continued Rate Cuts Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milian has announced that the central bank intends to proceed with interest rate cuts at the upcoming March meeting. According to NS3.AI, despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, the Federal Reserve's outlook remains steady. Milian emphasized the goal of reducing rates by 0.25% increments until a neutral interest rate level is achieved, at which point the situation will be reassessed.

data.title: Market news: The EU is expected to be exempted from the US tariffs, which have generally increased to 15%.
data.description: According to market sources, the EU is expected to be exempted from the tariffs that the US has generally increased to 15%. (Jinshi)
